Before the MSc

Foundations —
where I came from

Before enrolling at VU Amsterdam, I built my foundation across four very different environments. Each one added a layer of professional and personal awareness that I carried directly into this MSc year.

2019–2020
Level 4 Psychology · University of East London
Statistics, quantitative & qualitative analysis, behavioural studies. First encounter with academic rigour.
2020–2024
BA International & European Studies · University of Macedonia
Diplomacy specialisation. Thesis on EU border management and migration policy. Policy awareness and academic writing.
Feb–Jun 2024
Embassy of Greece · The Hague
Diplomatic protocol, confidential documentation, consular citizen services. Institutions affect real people.
Jan–Aug 2025
TaskUs · Coinbase Project
KYC, compliance, fraud risk, cross-cultural case management. High-volume, regulated fintech operations.
Ongoing
Social & civic engagement
Beach cleaning (Arkitsa), INEFAN non-profit workshops, ESN Thessaloniki. Staying connected beyond the career track.
Learning plan

My SMARTO goals —
and how this year measured up

01
Public speaking
Target: Significantly improve by July 2026
Approach: Presentations, oral exams, peer feedback, Student Ambassador role
Outcome: Multiple course presentations delivered. Appointed Student Ambassador — presented at VU recruitment events. Improvisation still developing.
In progress
02
Business English fluency
Target: Enhance command by June 2026
Approach: 2 business articles/week, essay writing, professional email communication
Outcome: Advanced through academic writing, thesis drafting, and daily professional communication at ABN AMRO. Reading habit less structured than planned.
In progress
03
Secure an internship
Target: By April 2026 in HR, project management, or operations
Approach: Targeted applications, career fairs, tailored CV and cover letters, networking
Outcome: Secured internship at ABN AMRO Bank N.V. — Leadership, Talent & Succession. Started January 2026. Exceeded original target in seniority and scope.
✓ Achieved
04
Clarify career direction
Target: By August 2026 — identify best-fit companies and roles
Approach: Analyse job descriptions and reviews, explore consulting/corporate operations
Outcome: Applied to ABN AMRO traineeship programmes in Talent & Leadership and Global Markets. Direction is more informed than settled — exploration continues with purpose.
Evolving
Self-reflection

How this year
shaped me

When I enrolled at VU Amsterdam in September 2025, my motivation was pragmatic and honest: I needed this degree to open doors. After working across three very different environments, I had a broad foundation but no clear professional identity. The MSc was my deliberate investment in building one — and the four SMARTO goals I set in October 2025 were my roadmap for doing so.

On imposter syndrome · ABN AMRO (SMARTO Goal 3)

Securing the ABN AMRO internship fulfilled Goal 3 — but arriving there confronted me with something I hadn’t anticipated: imposter syndrome. Surrounded by experienced professionals, I frequently felt underqualified. What changed was a deliberate shift: I focused on executing tasks well before trying to be impressive. Contribution doesn’t require complete confidence — it requires showing up prepared and asking good questions.

Three courses were pivotal: Leading Change and Projects demanded integrated thinking across governance and organisational dynamics. Advanced Leadership pushed me to critically engage competing theoretical models. Top Management required navigating political skill and executive complexity. These directly supported Goals 1 and 2.

On self-awareness · Recruitment & Selection (6.7)

This revealed an important pattern: I am more vulnerable to underperformance in areas where I feel prematurely comfortable. I underestimated the course and invested insufficient time. I now treat perceived familiarity as a signal for increased attention — a metacognitive adjustment I carry into every new challenge.

My role as International Student Ambassador gave me a structured space to work on Goal 1 directly — presenting to prospective students under a coordinator, representing a programme I was simultaneously studying. It turned a formal role into genuine practice.

Key takeaways

What I actually
learned this year

Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam
The MSc year
Academic writing & research
From structuring arguments to running moderated mediation models, I developed analytical rigour that did not exist before. The thesis was the hardest test.
Performance under pressure
Multiple simultaneous deadlines across 7 courses forced me to prioritise ruthlessly and still deliver quality output consistently.
Collaborating with strangers
Every group project paired me with people I had never met. Aligning quickly and still producing something exceptional is a professional skill in itself.
Critical & analytical thinking
The programme trained me to interrogate evidence, question assumptions, and resist premature conclusions.
Applied leadership knowledge
Leading projects, ethics, top management, HR — a working toolkit I arrived at ABN AMRO already carrying.
ABN AMRO Bank N.V.
The internship
Opportunity is earned, not given
Young, inexperienced, and non-Dutch — these are not disqualifiers if you show up prepared and prove your worth through consistent output.
Precision matters — and mistakes happen
Every deliverable demands care. A mistake can put your position at stake. But I learned to step back, take ownership, and rebuild trust through hard work.
How organisations actually run
Talent pools, Hay levels, manager evaluation frameworks, succession planning mechanics — knowledge unavailable in any classroom.
Stakeholder communication is the work
Working alongside consultants and ROs taught me that how you communicate often determines outcomes more than the content itself.
Leadership has a societal dimension
Talent management shapes organisations, which shape careers, which shape lives. This gave the internship work a sense of meaning beyond task completion.
Portfolio evidence

Skills in practice

01
ABN AMRO internship
Leadership development and succession initiatives. Benchmarking analyses, workshop briefing materials, cross-functional stakeholder coordination alongside senior consultants and ROs.
SMARTO goal link

Fulfils Goal 3 (internship by April 2026) — achieved ahead of schedule. Supports Goal 4 through direct exposure to corporate HR and talent management.

CommunicativeMetacognitive
02
Master’s thesis — Professional Language Barriers & Reluctance to Lead
Mediation-moderation research on professional language barriers and reluctance to lead. Leadership-specific self-doubt as mediator, leader prototypicality as moderator. JASP, Process Model 7.
SMARTO goal link

Supports Goal 2 (English fluency & business terminology) — full academic paper at postgraduate level.

CommunicativeSelf-insight
03
Leadership course deliverables
ING chatbot engagement redesign — Leading Change & Projects (8.6). IKEA multicultural leadership — Advanced Leadership (8.5). Company visit to ING HQ. Applied frameworks directly at ABN AMRO.
SMARTO goal link

Addresses Goal 1 (presentations delivered) and Goal 2 (analytical writing). ING and IKEA projects support Goal 4 through real corporate challenges.

Self-insightMetacognitiveCommunicative
04
International Student Ambassador
Appointed by VU coordinator Kathleen Stephenson as student assistant for the Master’s Event. Presentations to prospective MSc students, recruitment event representation, programme communication.
SMARTO goal link

Directly addresses Goal 1 (public speaking). The learning plan named this role as a fallback strategy — instead it became a primary vehicle for developing this skill ahead of schedule.

Communicative
05
Self-awareness in practice
Deloitte reference check redesign — Recruitment & Selection (6.7). Strong group work, but I underestimated the course individually. Revealed a key pattern: comfort ≠ performance. Perceived familiarity now triggers increased attention, not reduced effort.
SMARTO goal link

Demonstrates the metacognitive self-correction described across all four SMARTO goals: identifying when an approach is failing and adjusting deliberately.

Self-insightMetacognitive
06
Embassy of Greece · The Hague
Diplomatic administration, confidential documentation, citizen services under strict protocol. Professionalism in a high-stakes institutional environment. Every process has a human being at the end of it.
CommunicativeBroad horizon
